description: Letter from Nissim b. Yishaq al-Tāhartī. Around 1057. The letter was probably from Susa to Fustat and it contains the account of Musa b. Barhun al-Tāhartī after his death. The writer calls Musa “al-Marhum” – the one who has God’s mercy, a phrase that is taken from the Muslim culture. Mentions the year ד'תתט"ו but the letter is later than that. Mentions big changes in the Dinar rate vs. the dirham. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #391) VMR
description_he: מכתב מניסים בן יצחק אל-טאהרתי, כ-1057, כנראה מסוסה לפוסטאט, עם חשבון מוסא בן ברהון אל-טאהרתי לאחר מותו
